data.body: ROOT-CAUSE UPDATE #2 + CONFIRMED FIX (post dc33-v2 deploy). dc33 v2's freshen (git fetch origin tickets; git reset --hard FETCH_HEAD; run_ensures) resolves the ticket now, but the rebar.comment COMMIT fails: 'cannot lock ref HEAD: reference already exists'. DIAGNOSIS (confirmed on the live store): after reset --hard FETCH_HEAD the store is left with .git/HEAD -> refs/heads/.invalid and an EMPTY refs/heads/ (no refs/heads/tickets), because FETCH_HEAD reset never establishes a local branch (unlike the review-bot's 'clone --single-branch --branch tickets', which creates refs/heads/tickets + HEAD->it). rebar's commit machinery then cannot lock HEAD. CONFIRMED FIX: forcing 'git checkout -f -B tickets origin/tickets' (proper branch ref; -f to clear run_ensures' staged .gitattributes/.gitignore) makes rebar.comment SUCCEED (verified: COMMENT_OK on the live container). So the freshen (_freshen_ticket_store in infra/autolander/loop.py) + the Dockerfile entrypoint must end the store ON refs/heads/tickets via 'git checkout -f -B tickets origin/tickets' (or reset onto the branch after checking it out), NOT a detached 'reset --hard FETCH_HEAD'. This is the 4th and (expected) final layer of the annotation-store fix. dc33 remains OPEN pending this freshen fix + redeploy + a clean bot-landing annotation. NOTE: bot is PAUSED (emergency-stop) so no landing annotates meanwhile.
